req.title: System-Admin views department
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a department through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
Here are comprehensive pytest tests for the requirement:

```python
"""
Test Suite: View Department - System Admin UI Form
Requirement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a department
             through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an
             audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():
    """Authenticated system-admin user."""
    return MagicMock(
        id=1,
        username="sys_admin",
        role="system-admin",
        is_authenticated=True,
        is_active=True,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_user():
    """Authenticated user without system-admin role."""
    return MagicMock(
        id=2,
        username="regular_user",
        role="viewer",
        is_authenticated=True,
        is_active=True,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Unauthenticated / anonymous user."""
    return MagicMock(
        id=None,
        username=None,
        role=None,
        is_authenticated=False,
        is_active=False,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def valid_department():
    """Fully-populated department record."""
    return MagicMock(
        id=101,
        department_code="ENG-001",
        department_name="Engineering",
        description="Software Engineering Department",
        manager="Jane Doe",
        location="Building A",
        cost_center="CC-1001",
        status="Active",
        created_at=datetime(2023, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0),
        updated_at=datetime(2024, 3, 10, 14, 30, 0),
    )


@pytest.fixture
def department_missing_mandatory_fields():
    """Department record with mandatory fields set to None/empty."""
    return MagicMock(
        id=102,
        department_code=None,   # mandatory – missing
        department_name="",     # mandatory – empty
        description="Some description",
        manager=None,
        location=None,
        cost_center=None,
        status=None,            # mandatory – missing
        created_at=None,
        updated_at=None,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def audit_service():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def department_service(valid_department, department_missing_mandatory_fields):
    svc = MagicMock()
    svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = valid_department
    svc.validate_mandatory_fields.return_value = (True, [])
    return svc


@pytest.fixture
def department_form_view(system_admin_user, department_service, audit_service):
    """
    Simulated UI form view controller wiring together the collaborators.
    Replace this with your real import, e.g.
        from app.views.department import DepartmentFormView
    """
    from app.views.department import DepartmentFormView   # noqa: F401 – real import
    view = DepartmentFormView(
        user=system_admin_user,
        department_service=department_service,
        audit_service=audit_service,
    )
    return view


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – build the view under test without a real module (remove when
# the real module exists and the fixture above is activated).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class _DepartmentFormView:
    """
    Thin stand-in for the real DepartmentFormView.
    Mirrors the contract expected by the tests.
    """

    MANDATORY_FIELDS = ("department_code", "department_name", "status")

    def __init__(self, user, department_service, audit_service):
        self.user = user
        self._dept_svc = department_service
        self._audit_svc = audit_service

    # ------------------------------------------------------------------ #
    def view_department(self, department_id: int) -> dict:
        # 1. Authentication check
        if not self.user.is_authenticated:
            return {"success": False, "error": "UNAUTHENTICATED", "data": None}

        # 2. Authorisation check
        if self.user.role != "system-admin":
            return {"success": False, "error": "UNAUTHORISED", "data": None}

        # 3. Fetch record
        department = self._dept_svc.get_department_by_id(department_id)
        if department is None:
            return {"success": False, "error": "NOT_FOUND", "data": None}

        # 4. Mandatory-field validation
        is_valid, missing = self._dept_svc.validate_mandatory_fields(department)
        if not is_valid:
            return {
                "success": False,
                "error": "VALIDATION_FAILED",
                "missing_fields": missing,
                "data": None,
            }

        # 5. Write audit record
        self._audit_svc.write(
            actor=self.user.username,
            action="VIEW_DEPARTMENT",
            resource_id=department_id,
            timestamp=datetime.utcnow(),
        )

        return {"success": True, "error": None, "data": department}


# Override the fixture to use the stand-in (comment out when real module exists)
@pytest.fixture
def department_form_view(system_admin_user, department_service, audit_service):
    return _DepartmentFormView(
        user=system_admin_user,
        department_service=department_service,
        audit_service=audit_service,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Authentication Tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthentication:

    def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_department(
        self, unauthenticated_user, department_service, audit_service
    ):
        """Unauthenticated users must be denied access."""
        view = _DepartmentFormView(unauthenticated_user, department_service, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=101)

        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["error"] == "UNAUTHENTICATED"
        assert result["data"] is None

    def test_unauthenticated_user_does_not_trigger_audit(
        self, unauthenticated_user, department_service, audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record should be written for unauthenticated access attempts."""
        view = _DepartmentFormView(unauthenticated_user, department_service, audit_service)
        view.view_department(department_id=101)

        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_unauthenticated_user_does_not_call_department_service(
        self, unauthenticated_user, department_service, audit_service
    ):
        """Department service must not be invoked for unauthenticated requests."""
        view = _DepartmentFormView(unauthenticated_user, department_service, audit_service)
        view.view_department(department_id=101)

        department_service.get_department_by_id.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Authorisation Tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuthorisation:

    def test_non_admin_user_cannot_view_department(
        self, non_admin_user, department_service, audit_service
    ):
        """A user without the system-admin role must be denied access."""
        view = _DepartmentFormView(non_admin_user, department_service, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=101)

        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["error"] == "UNAUTHORISED"
        assert result["data"] is None

    def test_non_admin_user_does_not_trigger_audit(
        self, non_admin_user, department_service, audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record should be written for unauthorised access attempts."""
        view = _DepartmentFormView(non_admin_user, department_service, audit_service)
        view.view_department(department_id=101)

        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_system_admin_is_permitted(
        self, system_admin_user, department_service, audit_service
    ):
        """A system-admin must be allowed to proceed past the authorisation gate."""
        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, department_service, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=101)

        # Must not be blocked by auth/authz errors
        assert result["error"] not in ("UNAUTHENTICATED", "UNAUTHORISED")


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Successful View Tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestSuccessfulView:

    def test_view_department_returns_success(self, department_form_view):
        """A valid request by a system-admin must return a success response."""
        result = department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_view_department_returns_department_data(
        self, department_form_view, valid_department
    ):
        """The response payload must contain the department record."""
        result = department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        assert result["data"] is not None
        assert result["data"] == valid_department

    def test_view_department_calls_service_with_correct_id(
        self, department_form_view, department_service
    ):
        """The department service must be called with the requested ID."""
        department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        department_service.get_department_by_id.assert_called_once_with(101)

    def test_view_department_no_error_in_response(self, department_form_view):
        """A successful response must carry no error indicator."""
        result = department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        assert result["error"] is None

    def test_view_department_displays_all_mandatory_fields(
        self, department_form_view, valid_department
    ):
        """All mandatory fields must be present and non-empty in the returned data."""
        result = department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)
        dept = result["data"]

        assert dept.department_code not in (None, "")
        assert dept.department_name not in (None, "")
        assert dept.status not in (None, "")

    def test_view_department_displays_optional_fields(
        self, department_form_view, valid_department
    ):
        """Optional fields should also be surfaced when present."""
        result = department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)
        dept = result["data"]

        assert dept.description is not None
        assert dept.manager is not None
        assert dept.location is not None
        assert dept.cost_center is not None


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Mandatory Field Validation Tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:

    def test_validation_called_on_department_data(
        self, department_form_view, department_service
    ):
        """The mandatory-field validator must be invoked for every view attempt."""
        department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        department_service.validate_mandatory_fields.assert_called_once()

    def test_missing_department_code_fails_validation(
        self,
        system_admin_user,
        department_missing_mandatory_fields,
        audit_service,
    ):
        """A department with a missing department_code must fail validation."""
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = department_missing_mandatory_fields
        dept_svc.validate_mandatory_fields.return_value = (
            False,
            ["department_code", "department_name", "status"],
        )

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=102)

        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["error"] == "VALIDATION_FAILED"
        assert "department_code" in result["missing_fields"]

    def test_missing_department_name_fails_validation(
        self, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """A department with an empty department_name must fail validation."""
        dept = MagicMock(department_code="ENG-001", department_name="", status="Active")
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = dept
        dept_svc.validate_mandatory_fields.return_value = (False, ["department_name"])

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=102)

        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "department_name" in result["missing_fields"]

    def test_missing_status_fails_validation(
        self, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """A department with a missing status must fail validation."""
        dept = MagicMock(department_code="ENG-001", department_name="Engineering", status=None)
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = dept
        dept_svc.validate_mandatory_fields.return_value = (False, ["status"])

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=102)

        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "status" in result["missing_fields"]

    def test_validation_failure_does_not_write_audit(
        self, system_admin_user, department_missing_mandatory_fields, audit_service
    ):
        """An audit record must NOT be written when validation fails."""
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = department_missing_mandatory_fields
        dept_svc.validate_mandatory_fields.return_value = (
            False, ["department_code", "department_name", "status"]
        )

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        view.view_department(department_id=102)

        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_validation_failure_returns_list_of_missing_fields(
        self, system_admin_user, department_missing_mandatory_fields, audit_service
    ):
        """The response must enumerate which mandatory fields are missing."""
        expected_missing = ["department_code", "department_name", "status"]
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = department_missing_mandatory_fields
        dept_svc.validate_mandatory_fields.return_value = (False, expected_missing)

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=102)

        assert set(result["missing_fields"]) == set(expected_missing)

    def test_all_mandatory_fields_present_passes_validation(
        self, department_form_view, department_service
    ):
        """Validation must pass when all mandatory fields are populated."""
        department_service.validate_mandatory_fields.return_value = (True, [])

        result = department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        assert result["success"] is True
        assert result.get("missing_fields") is None


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Audit Record Tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestAuditRecord:

    def test_audit_record_written_on_success(
        self, department_form_view, audit_service
    ):
        """An audit record must be written after a successful view operation."""
        department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        audit_service.write.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor(
        self, department_form_view, audit_service, system_admin_user
    ):
        """The audit record must identify the acting user."""
        department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        _, kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("actor") == system_admin_user.username

    def test_audit_record_contains_action_type(
        self, department_form_view, audit_service
    ):
        """The audit record must capture the action type VIEW_DEPARTMENT."""
        department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        _, kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("action") == "VIEW_DEPARTMENT"

    def test_audit_record_contains_resource_id(
        self, department_form_view, audit_service
    ):
        """The audit record must reference the viewed department's ID."""
        department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        _, kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("resource_id") == 101

    def test_audit_record_contains_timestamp(
        self, department_form_view, audit_service
    ):
        """The audit record must include a timestamp."""
        department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        _, kwargs = audit_service.write.call_args
        assert kwargs.get("timestamp") is not None
        assert isinstance(kwargs.get("timestamp"), datetime)

    def test_audit_record_not_written_for_non_existent_department(
        self, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """No audit record must be written when the requested department is not found."""
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = None

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        view.view_department(department_id=999)

        audit_service.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_record_written_exactly_once_per_view(
        self, department_form_view, audit_service
    ):
        """Exactly one audit record must be created per successful view call."""
        department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)
        department_form_view.view_department(department_id=101)

        assert audit_service.write.call_count == 2

    def test_audit_record_written_for_different_departments(
        self, system_admin_user, audit_service, valid_department
    ):
        """Each successful view of a different department produces a distinct audit entry."""
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = valid_department
        dept_svc.validate_mandatory_fields.return_value = (True, [])

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        view.view_department(department_id=101)
        view.view_department(department_id=202)

        calls = audit_service.write.call_args_list
        resource_ids = [c[1]["resource_id"] for c in calls]
        assert resource_ids == [101, 202]


# ===========================================================================
# 6. Department Not Found Tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestDepartmentNotFound:

    def test_not_found_returns_failure(
        self, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """A request for a non-existent department must return a failure response."""
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = None

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=999)

        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["error"] == "NOT_FOUND"
        assert result["data"] is None

    def test_not_found_does_not_call_validator(
        self, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """Validation must not run when the department cannot be found."""
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = None

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        view.view_department(department_id=999)

        dept_svc.validate_mandatory_fields.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 7. Edge / Boundary Tests
# ===========================================================================

class TestEdgeCases:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("dept_id", [0, -1, -999])
    def test_invalid_department_id_returns_not_found(
        self, system_admin_user, audit_service, dept_id
    ):
        """Non-positive department IDs should return NOT_FOUND."""
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = None

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=dept_id)

        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["error"] == "NOT_FOUND"

    def test_department_service_exception_propagates(
        self, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """Unexpected service exceptions must propagate (not be silently swallowed)."""
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.side_effect = RuntimeError("DB unavailable")

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)

        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="DB unavailable"):
            view.view_department(department_id=101)

    def test_audit_service_exception_propagates(
        self, system_admin_user, department_service, audit_service
    ):
        """Audit write failures must not be silently suppressed."""
        audit_service.write.side_effect = IOError("Audit store unreachable")

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, department_service, audit_service)

        with pytest.raises(IOError, match="Audit store unreachable"):
            view.view_department(department_id=101)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("role", ["viewer", "editor", "manager", "auditor", "guest"])
    def test_non_system_admin_roles_are_denied(
        self, audit_service, department_service, role
    ):
        """Every role other than system-admin must be denied access."""
        user = MagicMock(
            id=10, username=f"user_{role}", role=role,
            is_authenticated=True, is_active=True
        )
        view = _DepartmentFormView(user, department_service, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=101)

        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["error"] == "UNAUTHORISED"

    def test_whitespace_only_department_name_fails_validation(
        self, system_admin_user, audit_service
    ):
        """A department_name containing only whitespace must fail mandatory validation."""
        dept = MagicMock(department_code="ENG-001", department_name="   ", status="Active")
        dept_svc = MagicMock()
        dept_svc.get_department_by_id.return_value = dept
        dept_svc.validate_mandatory_fields.return_value = (False, ["department_name"])

        view = _DepartmentFormView(system_admin_user, dept_svc, audit_service)
        result = view.view_department(department_id=101)

        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "department_name" in result["missing_fields"]
```

---

### What is covered

| # | Area | Tests |
|---|------|-------|
| 1 | **Authentication** | Unauthenticated user denied; service & audit not called |
| 2 | **Authorisation** | Non-admin roles denied; system-admin permitted |
| 3 | **Successful view** | Response shape, data integrity, service interaction |
| 4 | **Mandatory field validation** | Each mandatory field tested individually; validation called; missing fields enumerated |
| 5 | **Audit record** | Written on success only; contains actor, action, resource ID, timestamp; call count; not written on failure |
| 6 | **Not found** | 404-style path; validator not invoked; audit not written |
| 7 | **Edge cases** | Invalid IDs, service/audit exceptions, all non-admin roles parametrised, whitespace-only values |

```python
"""
Test Suite: View Department - System Admin UI Form
Requirement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to view a department
             through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an
             audit record must be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def system_admin_user():
    """Authenticated system-admin user."""
    return MagicMock(
        id=1,
        username="sys_admin",
        role="system-admin",
        is_authenticated=True,
        is_active=True,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def non_admin_user():
    """Authenticated user without system-admin role."""
    return MagicMock(
        id=2,
        username="regular_user",
        role="viewer",
        is_authenticated=True,
        is_active=True,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Unauthenticated / anonymous user."""
    return MagicMock(
        id=None,
        username=None,
        role=None,
        is_authenticated=False,
        is_active=False,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def valid_department():
    """Fully-populated department record."""
    return MagicMock(
        id=101,
        department_code="ENG-001",
        department_name="Engineering",
        description="Software Engineering Department",
        manager="Jane Doe",
        location="Building A",
        cost_center="CC-1001",
        status="Active",
        created_at=datetime(2023, 1, 15, 9, 0, 0),
        updated_at=datetime(2024, 3, 10, 14, 30, 0),
    )
